Fuel price hike: Can Centre shield OMCs with Rs 3 cushion, or will rupee flip the script?
description: The government increased fuel prices by Rs 3 per litre to support oil marketing companies facing losses due to high global crude oil prices and a weakening rupee. However, further rupee depreciation could negate these gains, with companies incurring significant daily losses. The price hike offers limited relief, covering only a fraction of projected losses.

‘Will erode goodwill’: Congress warns Vijay against inducting rebel AIADMK MLAs into Tamil Nadu govt
description: Congress has cautioned Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay against incorporating rebel AIADMK members into his government.  Karti Chidambaram stressed that the public mandate was for a government independent of both AIADMK and DMK.  Inducting the splinter group, he warned, would significantly damage Vijay's goodwill and credibility, urging him to remain true to the electoral mandate.

title: Operation Epic Fury to resume? Trump weighs fresh Iran strikes after China visit: Report
description: As US President Trump returns from China, pressure mounts over Iran. Top aides are reportedly preparing options for renewed military action against Tehran if diplomacy falters, including intensified bombing and potential Special Operations deployments.  Iran, meanwhile, signals readiness for conflict, while the Strait of Hormuz remains a critical concern for global oil prices.

title: Ex-Google CEO Eric Schmidt has message for engineers who are still coding the old way
description: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt declared the era of manual code writing is over, citing AI's explosive power. He believes programmers still using traditional methods are behind, as AI enables individuals to build powerful applications previously requiring large teams. This shift is causing a sell-off in software stocks, with companies needing to adapt quickly to survive.

title: After Microsoft gave engineers access to Anthropic's Claude Code, company is canceling licenses
description: Microsoft is phasing out internal licenses for Anthropic's Claude Code AI tool, shifting developers to its own GitHub Copilot CLI platform. This strategic move, effective by the end of June, aims to streamline AI coding tools and align with Microsoft's engineering needs and financial year-end.
